scope: End-to-end OpenClaude streamingToolUses + toolJSX parity for the AgenC TUI: live host (App.tsx), transcript producer (message-adapter.ts + use-session-transcript.ts), and Anthropic provider input_json_delta forwarding. Non-Anthropic providers (openai, grok, deepseek, gemini, groq, lmstudio, ollama, openrouter) are explicitly out of scope for this contract and will be tracked in a follow-up parity contract.

requiredBehaviors: App.tsx no longer hardcodes streamingToolUses={[]}; reads the array from transcript.streamingToolUses (sourced via use-session-transcript.ts) and passes it to <Messages>
Initial render with no in-flight tools passes an empty array, matching upstream REPL.tsx:853 initial state
Array identity is stable across re-renders when contents are unchanged (no new reference per render), matching the memoization Messages.tsx:732+ relies on
edgeCases: adaptTranscriptEvents([]) returns an AdaptedTranscript whose streamingToolUses field is an empty array (typeof === 'object' && length === 0)

Constructing an AdaptedTranscript with streamingToolUses set to a single-element array containing {index: 0, contentBlock: {type:'tool_use', id:'a', name:'X', input:{}}, unparsedToolInput: '{"q":'} preserves the element identity when read back
Constructing an AdaptedTranscript with two streamingToolUses entries at indices 0 and 1 preserves both elements with their distinct unparsedToolInput strings
App.tsx source no longer contains the literal hardcoded JSX 'streamingToolUses={[]}' (asserted by reading the file and matching with a non-trivial regex)
App.tsx source contains the wiring 'streamingToolUses={transcript.streamingToolUses}' (or an equivalent prop expression that references the transcript field)
message-adapter.ts imports StreamingToolUse from runtime/src/agenc/upstream/utils/messages.js so the AgenC transcript field type is identical to the upstream <Messages> consumer prop type

requiredBehaviors: App.tsx no longer hardcodes toolJSX={null}; declares useState<ToolJSXState | null>(null) where ToolJSXState matches REPL.tsx:1036 shape ({jsx: React.ReactNode | null, shouldHidePromptInput: boolean, shouldContinueAnimation?: true, showSpinner?: boolean, isLocalJSXCommand?: boolean, isImmediate?: boolean})
App.tsx ports the upstream localJSXCommandRef + setToolJSX wrapper from REPL.tsx:1055-1104 so local-JSX commands persist across normal tool updates and only clear on explicit clearLocalJSX:true
The wrapped setToolJSX is exposed to tools through the existing getToolUseContext callback so AgenC tools that render their own UI surfaces can call it
When toolJSX !== null, App.tsx renders <Box flexDirection="column" width="100%">{toolJSX.jsx}</Box> as a sibling block, mirroring REPL.tsx:4469-4470
The current toolJSX value is passed verbatim to <Messages toolJSX={...}>, not a normalized copy
edgeCases: applyToolJSXUpdate(prev=null, args={isLocalJSXCommand:true, jsx:'X', shouldHidePromptInput:false}) stores args (minus clearLocalJSX) in the local-ref equivalent and returns the args as the new state value
applyToolJSXUpdate when local-ref is set and args lacks clearLocalJSX returns the previous state unchanged (REPL.tsx:1095 'keep the local JSX command visible')
applyToolJSXUpdate when local-ref is set and args.clearLocalJSX===true returns null AND clears the local-ref
applyToolJSXUpdate when local-ref is null and args.clearLocalJSX===true returns null
applyToolJSXUpdate when local-ref is null and args is a normal value returns args as-is (no transformation)
applyToolJSXUpdate(prev, null) returns null when local-ref is null (full clear path)
App.tsx source no longer contains the literal hardcoded 'toolJSX={null}' on the <Messages> element; the prop is wired to the toolJSX state variable
App.tsx source contains the sibling Box rendering the toolJSX.jsx node when toolJSX is non-null (matches REPL.tsx:4469-4470 exactly)

requiredBehaviors: The toolJSX state value is passed verbatim into <Messages toolJSX={toolJSX}> so the upstream-mirrored animation gate at runtime/src/agenc/upstream/components/Messages.tsx:595 (canAnimate = (!toolJSX || !!toolJSX.shouldContinueAnimation) && !toolUseConfirmQueue.length && !isMessageSelectorVisible) actually receives the live toolJSX value (R2 wiring is the load-bearing change; this row asserts the gate is reachable)

Permission overlay rendering condition is unchanged (renders when permissionRequests[0] is truthy) — upstream parity here is 'dialog still renders when toolJSX is set' which AgenC already does, NOT a new gate

isMessageSelectorVisible passed to <Messages> is false (parity with AgenC's no-message-selector host) so the Messages animation gate's third clause never blocks animation due to a missing AgenC feature

toolUseConfirmQueue array passed to <Messages> reflects the live confirm queue from buildToolUseConfirmQueue(permissionRequests, tools) so the animation gate's second clause respects pending confirmations
edgeCases: Building a Messages.tsx canAnimate computation with toolJSX=null AND empty toolUseConfirmQueue AND isMessageSelectorVisible=false yields canAnimate=true (REPL.tsx:595)
Computation with toolJSX={shouldContinueAnimation:true} AND empty queue yields canAnimate=true (set-but-continuing path)
Computation with toolJSX={shouldContinueAnimation:undefined, jsx:'X', shouldHidePromptInput:false} AND empty queue yields canAnimate=false (set-and-blocking path)
Computation with toolJSX=null AND non-empty toolUseConfirmQueue yields canAnimate=false (queue-blocks path)
Computation with toolJSX=null AND empty queue AND isMessageSelectorVisible=true yields canAnimate=false (selector-blocks path)
App.tsx source passes the toolJSX state variable, an empty/derived toolUseConfirmQueue, and isMessageSelectorVisible={false} to <Messages> (asserted by reading App.tsx)

approvedDivergence.reason: Upstream REPL.tsx renders a host-level <SpinnerWithVerb> at line 4653 gated by REPL.tsx:1704 (showSpinner) and an idle notification timer at REPL.tsx:4005. AgenC's TUI host (runtime/src/tui/openclaude/App.tsx) renders neither of these surfaces today; PromptInput owns its own spinner-adjacent UI internally. Adding a host-level spinner and idle timer with full apiMetricsRef / responseLengthRef / streamMode plumbing is a separate parity concern (a future contract scoped to host-level streaming-status surfaces), and is therefore out of scope for the streaming-tool-use parity contract. The toolJSX-gated animation behavior is preserved through the upstream-mirrored <Messages> component (R2 wires it).
approvedDivergence.sourceBehavior: Upstream REPL renders <SpinnerWithVerb> with a showSpinner gate that combines toolJSX, toolUseConfirmQueue, promptQueue, and an idle notification timer that only fires when !isLoading && !toolJSX.
approvedDivergence.targetBehavior: AgenC App.tsx does not render a host-level Spinner or run an idle notification timer. The toolJSX-driven animation/dialog-gating behavior is preserved through the upstream-mirrored <Messages> component (animation gate at Messages.tsx:595) and through the OpenClaudePermissionOverlay (which already renders when there is a request, regardless of toolJSX).

requiredBehaviors: RunningToolProgressIndicator function and its <Text> rendering (App.tsx:111-146) are removed
<RunningToolProgressIndicator progress={transcript.runningToolProgress} /> JSX is removed from App.tsx render output
Imports of RunningToolProgress type from message-adapter are removed from App.tsx
runningToolProgress field is removed from OpenClaudeBridgeSession transcript shape and from message-adapter.ts (lines 42, 576, 706-734, 797)
RunningToolProgress type export is removed from message-adapter.ts; the row-reviewer subagent verifies via grep that no live caller outside dead-code paths references it before approving the row
edgeCases: After removal, an active tool stream still produces visible UI (proven by tui-host-streamingtooluses tests rendering synthetic cells in <Messages>)
No transcript consumer outside App.tsx and message-adapter.ts references runningToolProgress (verified via grep in the parity test)
TypeScript build succeeds with the field removed (no compile errors from stale references)
AgenC-specific RunningToolProgress field absence is asserted by an executable check that fails if the symbol reappears in App.tsx or transcript types

requiredBehaviors: message-adapter.ts builds a streamingToolUses: StreamingToolUse[] from the same input event stream it consumes today, using the upstream contract from messages.ts handleMessageFromStream (content_block_start + input_json_delta cases at messages.ts:3029, 3062-3079)
On content_block_start whose contentBlock.type === 'tool_use', append {index, contentBlock, unparsedToolInput: ''} to the array

On input_json_delta with a known index, replace the matching element with {...element, unparsedToolInput: element.unparsedToolInput + delta} (immutable update, matches upstream return shape at messages.ts:3071-3077)
On input_json_delta with an index that does not match any existing element, return the array unchanged (matches messages.ts:3068-3070 early return when find() yields no element)
On stream completion / new turn boundary AND on turn_aborted, the array is cleared (matches REPL.tsx:1609 and 2940 setStreamingToolUses([]) calls)
use-session-transcript.ts exposes streamingToolUses on the transcript snapshot shape consumed by App.tsx
StreamingToolUse type is imported from runtime/src/agenc/upstream/utils/messages.ts so the AgenC bridge type matches the upstream <Messages> consumer prop type exactly
edgeCases: Empty input event sequence yields streamingToolUses=[]
Single tool with a single delta yields one element with the delta concatenated into unparsedToolInput
Two concurrent tools at indices 0 and 1 with interleaved deltas yield two elements each containing only their own deltas
Out-of-order delta (delta arrives before content_block_start for that index) does not throw and does not create a phantom element — array unchanged
Delta with empty partial_json string is appended as the empty string (no-op concat, no exception)
Stream cancelled mid-delta (turn_aborted event) clears the array; subsequent stream starts from empty
Transcript snapshot identity changes when streamingToolUses changes (new reference) but is stable across re-derivations with the same input
Multi-megabyte cumulative unparsedToolInput does not regex over the whole string per delta (parity with upstream perf — handler does immutable concat only, no full-string scans)

requiredBehaviors: LLMStreamChunk in runtime/src/llm/types.ts gains optional toolInputBlockStart and toolInputDelta fields whose payloads carry {callId, index, contentBlock} and {callId, index, partialJson} respectively, mirroring the upstream content_block_start (tool_use) and input_json_delta data shapes so the AgenC TUI bridge can reconstruct the upstream streamingToolUses contract from messages.ts:3024-3079

The Anthropic adapter (adapter.ts) emits a chunk with toolInputBlockStart whenever it receives an SSE content_block_start whose content_block.type === 'tool_use' and a non-negative index — fired BEFORE any input_json_delta for that block reaches the consumer

The Anthropic adapter emits a chunk with toolInputDelta for each content_block_delta whose delta.type === 'input_json_delta' and a non-negative index, carrying the raw partial_json string, in addition to (not in place of) its existing toolBlocks.arguments accumulation
Forwarded events do not break the existing completed-tool-call emission at content_block_stop (current behavior at adapter.ts ~lines 292-311 must still emit chunk.toolCalls)
Existing onChunk text-delta path is unaffected (no regression in streaming text rendering)

stream-model.ts onChunk handler emits a session event {type:'tool_input_block_start', payload:{callId,index,contentBlock}} when chunk.toolInputBlockStart is set, and a session event {type:'tool_input_delta', payload:{callId,index,partialJson}} when chunk.toolInputDelta is set; both feed the message-adapter accumulator from R5

event-log.ts EventMsg discriminated union and KNOWN_EVENT_TYPES set declare both tool_input_block_start and tool_input_delta variants so the typed Session.emit accepts the new events without falling into the I-26 forward-compat shim path used for unrecognized event tags
edgeCases: An SSE content_block_start with content_block.type !== 'tool_use' does NOT emit toolInputBlockStart (only tool_use blocks open a streaming-tool-use entry; text blocks must not appear in transcript.streamingToolUses)
An SSE content_block_start with index === -1 (the adapter's sentinel for missing index) does NOT emit toolInputBlockStart (only valid indices reach the bridge)
An input_json_delta whose partial_json is not a string is dropped at the adapter (matches the existing typeof guard at adapter.ts:285); no chunk.toolInputDelta is emitted
Multiple input_json_deltas for the same tool block in rapid succession all forward through onChunk in arrival order with their partial_json contents preserved
Two concurrent tool_use blocks at distinct indices each emit their own toolInputBlockStart and their own toolInputDelta chunks without interleaving payloads
A consumer that ignores chunk.toolInputBlockStart and chunk.toolInputDelta still observes the existing completed-tool-call emission at content_block_stop (backward-compatibility)
stream-model.ts onChunk handler does NOT emit a session event when chunk.toolInputBlockStart and chunk.toolInputDelta are both undefined (only the existing assistant-text and tool-call paths fire)

The new chunk fields are opt-in for non-Anthropic providers — providers that do not set them produce no streaming-tool-use UI, but their existing tool-call emission is unaffected (matches the matrix scope statement that openai/grok/etc. are out of scope for this contract)

approvedExceptions.reason: TypeScript 'unknown' keyword used in pre-existing type annotations and 'as unknown as' casts across the AgenC TUI bridge, the Anthropic provider, and the shared LLM/session type files. Legitimate TS language feature, not a contract shortcut. The checker's /\bunknown\b/i pattern conflates these with the 'unknown' shortcut term.

Pre-existing module-header JSDoc in stream-model.ts uses the word 'deferred' in 'the actual continuation decision is deferred to the boundary check' — describing real budget-tracker behavior, not a contract shortcut. The matrix scope does not require rewriting unrelated pre-existing prose.

The token 'partial' has multiple legitimate non-shortcut meanings in the touched files: a status enum value in event-log.ts; the TS Partial<T> utility type in test files; the noun 'partial JSON' / 'partial reply' / 'partial content' in adapter, types, and message-adapter comments; and the JSDoc field name 'partial' on LLMResponseError. None of these are contract shortcuts; rewriting them would damage real domain language.

applyToolJSXUpdate (use-tool-jsx.ts) returns a discriminated-union result whose first variant is { skip: true } — an explicit signal mirroring the upstream wrapper's 'keep the local JSX command visible — ignore tool updates' branch at REPL.tsx:1095. Tests reference 'skip' by name. Not a contract shortcut.

stream-model parity test uses the local variable name 'stub' (e.g. 'const stub = { emit, nextInternalSubId }') for the Session test double. This is a test-fixture variable name, not a marker of a stubbed implementation in production code.

App.tooljsx-gating.parity.test.tsx uses 'readonly unknown[]' as a type for toolUseConfirmQueue (TS keyword, not a shortcut), and a comment that quotes the upstream phrase 'show unless blocked by toolJSX' which contains the token 'blocked' from the FORBIDDEN_PATTERNS list. Both usages are legitimate references to upstream behavior, not contract shortcuts.

Pre-existing JSDoc comment in the RunningToolProgressIndicator block (App.tsx ~lines 95-113) contains 'not yet'. The entire comment block is removed by row tui-remove-runningtoolprogressindicator (R4) of this contract; the exception keeps the row checker green for R1/R2/R3 while R4 has not yet shipped.
